content.text: Found it — everything's in the `spark-runtime` namespace. Let me set context and dig deeper into each resource.

input.command:
kubectl config set-context --current --namespace=spark-runtime 2>/dev/null; NS=spark-runtime
echo "=== Pods ===" && kubectl -n $NS get pods -o wide
echo "=== ConfigMap spark-config ===" && kubectl -n $NS get configmap spark-config -o yaml
echo "=== Secret spark-credentials ===" && kubectl -n $NS get secret spark-credentials -o yaml
echo "=== monitor deploy image ===" && kubectl -n $NS get deployment spark-monitor -o yaml | grep -A5 "image:"

echo "=== spark-monitor deployment full ===" 
kubectl -n $NS get deployment spark-monitor -o yaml

input.command:
NS=spark-runtime
echo "=== spark-data-processor job ===" 
kubectl -n $NS get job spark-data-processor -o yaml
echo "=== spark-batch-processor job ===" 
kubectl -n $NS get job spark-batch-processor -o yaml

content.content:
=== spark-data-processor job ===
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  annotations:
    kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration: |
      {"apiVersion":"batch/v1","kind":"Job","metadata":{"annotations":{},"name":"spark-data-processor","namespace":"spark-runtime"},"spec":{"backoffLimit":0,"suspend":true,"template":{"metadata":{"labels":{"app":"spark-data-processor"}},"spec":{"containers":[{"command":["/bin/bash","-c","echo \"Running spark data processor\"\nexport SPARK_LOCAL_IP=$(hostname -i)\n/opt/spark/bin/spark-submit \\\n  --master local[1] \\\n  --class org.apache.spark.examples.SparkPi \\\n  /opt/spark/examples/jars/spark-examples_2.12-3.5.3.jar \\\n  200\n"],"image":"apache/spark:3.5.3","name":"spark-data-processor","resources":{"limits":{"cpu":"500m","memory":"1Gi"},"requests":{"cpu":"200m","memory":"512Mi"}}}],"restartPolicy":"Never","serviceAccountName":"spark"}}}}
  creationTimestamp: "2026-07-10T00:02:04Z"
  generation: 1
  labels:
    app: spark-data-processor
    batch.kubernetes.io/controller-uid: ae25b359-477b-4dba-bfde-3579ee11dfa8
    batch.kubernetes.io/job-name: spark-data-processor
    controller-uid: ae25b359-477b-4dba-bfde-3579ee11dfa8
    job-name: spark-data-processor
  name: spark-data-processor
  namespace: spark-runtime
  resourceVersion: "2764745"
  uid: ae25b359-477b-4dba-bfde-3579ee11dfa8
spec:
  backoffLimit: 0
  completionMode: NonIndexed
  completions: 1
  manualSelector: false
  parallelism: 1
  podReplacementPolicy: TerminatingOrFailed
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      batch.kubernetes.io/controller-uid: ae25b359-477b-4dba-bfde-3579ee11dfa8
  suspend: true
  template:
    metadata:
      creationTimestamp: null
      labels:
        app: spark-data-processor
        batch.kubernetes.io/controller-uid: ae25b359-477b-4dba-bfde-3579ee11dfa8
        batch.kubernetes.io/job-name: spark-data-processor
        controller-uid: ae25b359-477b-4dba-bfde-3579ee11dfa8
        job-name: spark-data-processor
    spec:
      containers:
      - command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - |
          echo "Running spark data processor"
          export SPARK_LOCAL_IP=$(hostname -i)
          /opt/spark/bin/spark-submit \
            --master local[1] \
            --class org.apache.spark.examples.SparkPi \
            /opt/spark/examples/jars/spark-examples_2.12-3.5.3.jar \
            200
        image: apache/spark:3.5.3
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        name: spark-data-processor
        resources:
          limits:
            cpu: 500m
            memory: 1Gi
          requests:
            cpu: 200m
            memory: 512Mi
        terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
        terminationMessagePolicy: File
      dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst
      restartPolicy: Never
      schedulerName: default-scheduler
      securityContext: {}
      serviceAccount: spark
      serviceAccountName: spark
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30
status:
  conditions:
  - lastProbeTime: "2026-07-10T00:02:04Z"
    lastTransitionTime: "2026-07-10T00:02:04Z"
    message: Job suspended
    reason: JobSuspended
    status: "True"
    type: Suspended
  ready: 0
  terminating: 0
  uncountedTerminatedPods: {}

content.text:
Now I understand the full picture. Let me fix each issue.

**1. Fix spark-monitor deployment image (rollback to working revision):**

input.command:
NS=spark-runtime
kubectl -n $NS rollout history deployment/spark-monitor
kubectl -n $NS set image deployment/spark-monitor spark-monitor=busybox:1.36
kubectl -n $NS rollout status deployment/spark-monitor --timeout=60s

description:
NS=spark-runtime
kubectl -n $NS rollout history deployment/spark-monitor
kubectl -n $NS set image deployment/spark-monitor spark-monitor=busybox:1.36
kubectl -n $NS rollout status deployment/spark-monitor --timeout=60s

summary:
NS=spark-runtime
kubectl -n $NS rollout history deployment/spark-monitor
kubectl -n $NS set image deployment/spark-monitor spark-monitor=busybox:1.36
kubectl -n $NS rollout status deployment/spark-monitor --timeout=60s

content.content:
deployment.apps/spark-monitor 
REVISION  CHANGE-CAUSE
1         v1 - working baseline image
2         v2 - broken image (ImagePullBackOff)

deployment.apps/spark-monitor image updated
Waiting for deployment spec update to be observed...
Waiting for deployment spec update to be observed...
Waiting for deployment "spark-monitor" rollout to finish: 1 old replicas are pending termination...
deployment "spark-monitor" successfully rolled out

Waiting for deployment spec update to be observed...
Waiting for deployment spec update to be observed...

deployment "spark-monitor" successfully rolled out

content.text: **2. Fix the Secret's expired API key:**

input.command:
NS=spark-runtime
kubectl -n $NS patch secret spark-credentials --type='json' -p='[{"op":"replace","path":"/data/api-key","value":"'"$(echo -n 'valid-api-key-active-2026' | base64 -w0)"'"}]'
kubectl -n $NS get secret spark-credentials -o jsonpath='{.data.api-key}' | base64 -d; echo

kubectl -n $NS get secret spark-credentials -o jsonpath='{.data.api-key}' | base64 -d; echo

kubectl -n $NS get secret spark-credentials -o jsonpath='{.data.api-key}' | base64 -d; echo

NS=spark-runtime
kubectl -n $NS patch configmap spark-config --type merge -p '{"data":{"spark.executor.memory":"512m"}}'
kubectl -n $NS get configmap spark-config -o yaml

content.text: **4. Un-suspend both Jobs so they run:**

input.command:
NS=spark-runtime
kubectl -n $NS patch job spark-data-processor --type merge -p '{"spec":{"suspend":false}}'
kubectl -n $NS patch job spark-batch-processor --type merge -p '{"spec":{"suspend":false}}'
sleep 5
kubectl -n $NS get jobs
kubectl -n $NS get pods

sleep 5
kubectl -n $NS get jobs
kubectl -n $NS get pods

content.text: Jobs are now running. Let me watch them to completion and verify the configmap doesn't get reverted by the external process.

input.command:
NS=spark-runtime
kubectl -n $NS wait --for=condition=complete job/spark-data-processor --timeout=120s &
PID1=$!
kubectl -n $NS wait --for=condition=complete job/spark-batch-processor --timeout=120s &
PID2=$!
wait $PID1
wait $PID2
echo "=== jobs ==="
kubectl -n $NS get jobs
echo "=== configmap check ==="
kubectl -n $NS get configmap spark-config -o yaml
